1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"strings"
6	)
7	
8	// PreClaimIntakeOutcome classifies the pre-claim intake decision for a bead.
9	// Only actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim; intake_error fail-opens.
10	type PreClaimIntakeOutcome string
11	
12	const (
13		PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic       PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_atomic"
14		PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "actionable_but_rewritten"
15		PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed     PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "too_large_decomposed"
16		PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired       PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "operator_required"
17		PreClaimIntakeError                  PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "intake_error"
18	)
19	
20	// PreClaimIntakeResult is the typed decision returned by the intake hook.
21	// Detail carries the human-readable rationale or infra failure context.
22	type PreClaimIntakeResult struct {
23		Outcome      PreClaimIntakeOutcome `json:"outcome,omitempty"`
24		Reason       string                `json:"reason,omitempty"`
25		SystemReason string                `json:"system_reason,omitempty"`
26		Detail       string                `json:"detail,omitempty"`
27		Rewrite      PreClaimIntakeRewrite `json:"rewrite,omitempty"`
28		// Decomposition is populated when Outcome == too_large_decomposed and the
29		// hook produced a concrete split with children and AC map.
30		Decomposition *PreClaimDecomposition `json:"decomposition,omitempty"`
31	}
32	
33	// PreClaimDecompositionChild is one proposed child bead in a splitter result.
34	type PreClaimDecompositionChild struct {
35		Title       string   `json:"title"`
36		Description string   `json:"description"`
37		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance"`
38		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
39	}
40	
41	// ACMapEntry maps one parent acceptance criterion to child coverage.
42	// Coverage must be non-empty; use "operator_required" or "non_scope" for
43	// ACs that cannot be expressed as a child bead.
44	type ACMapEntry struct {
45		ParentAC string `json:"parent_ac"` // text of the parent AC
46		Coverage string `json:"coverage"`  // child AC text | "operator_required" | "non_scope"
47	}
48	
49	// PreClaimDecomposition is the splitter output when outcome is too_large_decomposed.
50	// Every parent AC must have a corresponding ACMap entry with non-empty Coverage;
51	// a split with missing or empty Coverage entries is lossy and blocks for operator.
52	type PreClaimDecomposition struct {
53		Children  []PreClaimDecompositionChild `json:"children"`
54		ACMap     []ACMapEntry                 `json:"ac_map"`
55		Rationale string                       `json:"rationale"`
56	}
57	
58	// isDecompositionLossy returns true if any ACMapEntry has empty Coverage,
59	// indicating the split does not fully cover all parent acceptance criteria.
60	func isDecompositionLossy(acMap []ACMapEntry) bool {
61		for _, entry := range acMap {
62			if strings.TrimSpace(entry.Coverage) == "" {
63				return true
64			}
65		}
66		return false
67	}
68	
69	// PreClaimIntakeRewrite carries intent-preserving bead edits returned by the
70	// intake hook when a bead is actionable but needs safe refinement before
71	// claim.
72	type PreClaimIntakeRewrite struct {
73		Description   string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
74		Acceptance    string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
75		ChangedFields []string `json:"changed_fields,omitempty"`
76	}
77	
78	// PreClaimIntakeHook classifies a ready bead before Claim. The loop treats
79	// actionable_atomic as pass-through, skips Claim for non-atomic outcomes, and
80	// fail-opens when the hook returns an infrastructure error.
81	type PreClaimIntakeHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error)
82	
83	func (r PreClaimIntakeResult) normalizedOutcome() PreClaimIntakeOutcome {
84		switch r.Outcome {
85		case "", PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic:
86			return PreClaimIntakeActionableAtomic
87		case PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten, PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed,
88			PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired, PreClaimIntakeError:
89			return r.Outcome
90		default:
91			return PreClaimIntakeError
92		}
93	}

1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5		"strings"
6		"time"
7	)
8	
9	// Bead represents a portable work item with metadata.
10	// The schema matches bd/br JSONL format for interchange compatibility.
11	// Unknown fields from external sources are preserved in Extra.
12	type Bead struct {
13		ID        string    `json:"id"`
14		Title     string    `json:"title"`
15		Status    string    `json:"status"`
16		Priority  int       `json:"priority"`
17		IssueType string    `json:"issue_type"`
18		Owner     string    `json:"owner,omitempty"`
19		CreatedAt time.Time `json:"created_at"`
20		CreatedBy string    `json:"created_by,omitempty"`
21		UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
22	
23		// Optional fields (bd-compatible)
24		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
25		Parent      string   `json:"parent,omitempty"`
26		Description string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
27		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
28		Notes       string   `json:"notes,omitempty"`
29	
30		// Dependencies use bd-compatible format
31		Dependencies []Dependency `json:"dependencies,omitempty"`
32	
33		// Extra holds unknown fields for round-trip preservation.
34		// Workflow-specific fields (e.g. HELIX spec-id, execution-eligible)
35		// are stored here and written back on save.
36		Extra map[string]any `json:"-"`
37	}
38	
39	// Dependency represents a link between two beads (bd-compatible format).
40	type Dependency struct {
41		IssueID     string `json:"issue_id"`
42		DependsOnID string `json:"depends_on_id"`
43		Type        string `json:"type"` // "blocks", "related", etc.

13	// newBeadAcCheckCommand wires `ddx bead ac-check <id>` — mechanical per-AC
14	// verification (test-name match, symbol grep, negative-AC absence, mechanical
15	// file detection). Prose AC items yield needs_judgment, never auto-pass or
16	// auto-fail. The output is consumed by the reviewer at the gate so it adjudicates
17	// structured input instead of grepping a raw diff.
18	func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadAcCheckCommand() *cobra.Command {
19		cmd := &cobra.Command{
20			Use:   "ac-check <bead-id>",
21			Short: "Mechanically verify acceptance criteria against the working tree",
22			Long: `ac-check classifies each AC into a kind (test-name | build-gate |
23	negative | symbol | mechanical | prose) and gathers evidence
24	mechanically. Output is emitted as JSON to stdout (when --json) and to
25	.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when --attempt-id is given.
26	
27	Examples:
28	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123
29	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --rev-range main..HEAD --json
30	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --attempt-id 20260510T210000-abcdef00`,
31			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
32			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33				beadID := args[0]
34				s := f.beadStore()
35				b, err := s.Get(beadID)
36				if err != nil {
37					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
38				}
39				if b == nil {
40					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s not found", beadID)
41				}
42	
43				items := accheck.ParseAcceptance(b.Acceptance)
44				if len(items) == 0 {
45					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s has no acceptance criteria to check", beadID)
46				}
47	
48				revRange, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("rev-range")
49				attemptID, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("attempt-id")
50				asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
51				packages, _ := cmd.Flags().GetStringSlice("package")
52	
53				workspace := f.beadWorkspaceRoot()
54				if workspace == "" {
55					workspace = f.WorkingDir
56				}
57	
58				ctx := accheck.Context{
59					WorkingDir: workspace,
60					RevBase:    revRange,
61					Packages:   packages,
62					RunTest:    accheck.DefaultRunTest(workspace),
63					GitGrep:    accheck.DefaultGitGrep(workspace),
64					DiffHits:   accheck.DefaultDiffHits(workspace, revRange),
65				}
66	
67				entries := accheck.Evaluate(items, ctx)
68				out := accheck.Aggregate(beadID, attemptID, entries)
69	
70				// Persist to .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when we have one.
71				if attemptID != "" {
72					path := filepath.Join(workspace, ".ddx", "executions", attemptID, "ac-check.json")
73					if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
74						return fmt.Errorf("mkdir %s: %w", filepath.Dir(path), err)
75					}
76					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
77					if mErr != nil {
78						return fmt.Errorf("marshal ac-check.json: %w", mErr)
79					}
80					if err := os.WriteFile(path, blob, 0o644); err != nil {
81						return fmt.Errorf("write %s: %w", path, err)
82					}
83				}
84	
85				if asJSON {
86					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
87					if mErr != nil {
88						return mErr
89					}
90					fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout(), string(blob))
91					return nil
92				}
93				return accheck.WriteHuman(cmd.OutOrStdout(), out)
94			},
95		}
96		cmd.Flags().String("rev-range", "", "git diff range used to scope DiffHits (e.g., main..HEAD)")
97		cmd.Flags().String("attempt-id", "", "if set, persist ac-check.json under .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/")
98		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "emit JSON to stdout instead of the human-readable table")
99		cmd.Flags().StringSlice("package", nil, "Go packages to scope test runs to (default ./...)")
100		return cmd
101	}

12	// runHook executes a validation hook if it exists.
13	// Hook receives bead JSON on stdin. Exit codes:
14	//
15	//	0 = ok, 1 = hard error (stderr = message), 2 = warning (stderr printed, proceeds)
16	func (s *Store) runHook(name string, b *Bead) error {
17		hookPath := filepath.Join(s.Dir, "hooks", name)
18	
19		info, err := os.Stat(hookPath)
20		if os.IsNotExist(err) {
21			return nil // no hook installed
22		}
23		if err != nil {
24			return fmt.Errorf("bead: hook stat: %w", err)
25		}
26		if info.IsDir() {
27			return nil
28		}
29		// Check executable
30		if info.Mode()&0o111 == 0 {
31			return nil // not executable
32		}
33	
34		data, err := marshalBead(*b)
35		if err != nil {
36			return fmt.Errorf("bead: hook marshal: %w", err)
37		}
38	
39		cmd := exec.Command(hookPath)
40		cmd.Stdin = bytes.NewReader(data)
41		var stderr bytes.Buffer
42		cmd.Stderr = &stderr
43	
44		runErr := cmd.Run()
45		if runErr == nil {
46			return nil // exit 0 = ok
47		}
48	
49		exitErr, ok := runErr.(*exec.ExitError)
50		if !ok {
51			return fmt.Errorf("bead: hook %s: %w", name, runErr)
52		}
53	
54		msg := strings.TrimSpace(stderr.String())
55		if msg == "" {
56			msg = "validation failed"
57		}
58	
59		switch exitErr.ExitCode() {
60		case 1:
61			return fmt.Errorf("bead: hook %s: %s", name, msg)
62		case 2:
63			// Warning — print but don't block
64			fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "bead: hook %s warning: %s\n", name, msg)
65			return nil
66		default:
67			return fmt.Errorf("bead: hook %s exit %d: %s", name, exitErr.ExitCode(), msg)
68		}
69	}

## Research Summary: DDx Repository - Pre-Claim AC Quality Lint Feature

I've completed a thorough review of the DDx repository to understand the codebase structure for implementing a pre-claim AC quality lint feature. Here are the key findings:

### 1. **PreClaim Readiness Check Framework** (`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake.go`)

The pre-claim intake system classifies readiness outcomes:
- `actionable_atomic` - pass through directly to Claim
- `actionable_but_rewritten` - safe refinement before claim
- `too_large_decomposed` - decomposition suggested with ACMap coverage
- `operator_required` - ambiguous/scope issues
- `intake_error` - infrastructure failure (fail-opens)

**Key Types:**
- `PreClaimIntakeResult` - typed decision with Outcome, Reason, Detail, Rewrite, Decomposition
- `PreClaimDecomposition` - contains children + ACMapEntry array (parent AC → coverage mapping)
- `PreClaimIntakeHook` - function signature: `func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error)`

### 2. **AC Check Tool** (`cli/internal/bead/accheck/`)

The `accheck` package provides mechanical AC verification:

**Classification Kinds:**
- `KindTestName` - Go test identifiers (TestFoo)
- `KindBuildGate` - lefthook, go test ./..., pre-commit
- `KindNegative` - absence verification (no longer, does not)
- `KindSymbol` - backtick symbols (`SymbolName`) with presence + diff hits
- `KindMechanical` - rename, relocate, documentation changes
- `KindProse` - default (never auto-pass or auto-fail, always needs_judgment)

**Key Functions:**
- `ParseAcceptance(s string) []Item` - splits numbered/lettered AC lines
- `Evaluate(items []Item, ctx Context) []Entry` - mechanical evaluation
- `Aggregate(beadID, attemptID string, entries []Entry) Output` - summary stats
- `WriteHuman(w io.Writer, o Output)` - human-readable table output

**Output Schema (SchemaVersion 1):**
```go
type Output struct {
    SchemaVersion int
    BeadID        string
    AttemptID     string
    Summary       Summary // pass/fail/needs_judgment/error counts
    Items         []Entry // per-AC results
}

type Entry struct {
    AC       int
    Kind     Kind
    Name     string
    Result   Result // "pass" | "fail" | "needs_judgment" | "error"
    Evidence string
}
```

### 3. **Bead Structure** (`cli/internal/bead/types.go`)

```go
type Bead struct {
    ID, Title, Status, Priority, IssueType, Owner, CreatedBy string
    CreatedAt, UpdatedAt time.Time
    
    // Optional fields
    Labels       []string     // metadata tags
    Parent       string       // parent bead ID
    Description  string       // detailed context
    Acceptance   string       // numbered/lettered AC items
    Notes        string       // additional notes
    Dependencies []Dependency // depends_on relationships
    
    Extra        map[string]any // custom fields (round-trip preserved)
}

type BeadEvent struct {
    Kind      string    // event classifier
    Summary   string    // short summary
    Body      string    // detailed body
    Actor     string    // who triggered it
    CreatedAt time.Time
    Source    string    // source identification
}
```

### 4. **Existing AC-Check Command** (`cli/cmd/bead_ac_check.go`)

Implementation pattern:
```go
func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadAcCheckCommand() *cobra.Command {
    // Flags: --rev-range, --attempt-id, --json, --package
    // Output to .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json (if attempt-id provided)
    // Uses accheck.ParseAcceptance, accheck.Evaluate, accheck.Aggregate
    // Hooks: DefaultRunTest, DefaultGitGrep, DefaultDiffHits
}
```

### 5. **Bead Command Structure** (`cli/cmd/bead.go`)

The bead command family is organized as subcommands:
- `bead init`, `bead create`, `bead show`, `bead update`, `bead close`
- `bead list`, `bead ready`, `bead blocked`
- `bead ac-check` (existing)
- `bead dep add/remove/tree`
- `bead evidence add/list`
- `bead review`, `bead metrics`, `bead doctor`
- `bead migrate`, `bead archive`, etc.

**Auto-Commit Pattern:**
```go
// After mutations, always call:
f.beadAutoCommit("operation " + beadID)  // staged .ddx/beads.jsonl
```

### 6. **Event System** (`cli/internal/bead/store.go`)

Events are append-only evidence:
```go
func (s *Store) AppendEvent(id string, event BeadEvent) error
func (s *Store) Events(id string) ([]BeadEvent, error)
func (s *Store) EventsByKind(id, kind string) ([]BeadEvent, error)
```

Events stored in bead.Extra["events"] with truncation marker at 8KB. Externalized sidecar support available.

### 7. **Label System**

Labels are used for lifecycle state (legacy migration context):
- `LabelNeedsHuman` - operator attention required
- `LabelNeedsInvestigation` - investigation flag
- Custom labels: `kind:operator-prompt`, `source:web-ui`, `action:review`, `phase:review`

### 8. **PreClaim Intake Hook Integration** (`cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go`)

The hook is wired in the execute-loop:
```go
intakeHook := agent.NewPreClaimIntakeHook(projectRoot, store, rcfg, nil, qualityRunner)
// Used as: PreClaimIntakeHook: intakeHook
```

The hook implementation (`cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go`):
- Builds prompt from bead metadata + prior attempts
- Dispatches to agent for complexity evaluation
- Decodes result (supports multiple schema versions)
- Returns typed PreClaimIntakeResult

### 9. **Config Structure** (`cli/internal/config/config.go`)

Configuration loads from `.ddx/config.yaml`:
- Version field required
- Agent routing, library, triage policies
- Database connections, federation settings

### 10. **File Paths**

Key directories:
- `.ddx/beads.jsonl` - bead tracker (JSONL format)
- `.ddx/config.yaml` - DDx configuration
- `.ddx/hooks/` - executable validation hooks
- `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` - execution artifacts
- `cli/internal/bead/accheck/` - AC check package
- `cli/cmd/bead*.go` - bead command implementations
- `cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake*.go` - intake hook

### Implementation Strategy for AC Quality Lint

The feature should:

1. **Create `cli/cmd/bead_ac_lint.go`** following the pattern of `bead_ac_check.go`
   - Command: `ddx bead ac-lint <id>` 
   - Flags: `--attempt-id`, `--json`, `--strict` (to control fail vs warning)
   - Output JSON: `{checks: [{rule, severity, message, fix}], pass_count, warning_count, error_count}`

2. **Create quality checks in accheck package** (`cli/internal/bead/accheck/lint.go`)
   - Each check validates AC structure/clarity
   - Returns structured violations with hints
   - Examples: AC formatting, symbol naming, prose detection, acceptance completeness

3. **Integrate with pre-claim intake** as an optional _readiness_ quality gate
   - Wire in `agent_cmd.go` alongside PreDispatchLintHook pattern
   - Can emit events: `s.AppendEvent(beadID, BeadEvent{Kind: "ac-lint-quality", ...})`

4. **Use Extra field for lint metadata** when needed
   - `b.Extra["ac-lint-ignored"]` - rules to skip
   - `b.Extra["ac-lint-waived"]` - rule violations acknowledged

All file paths are absolute. Full file contents provided above for all key source files.

8	// PreClaimIntakeOutcome classifies the pre-claim intake decision for a bead.

15		PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed     PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "too_large_decomposed"
16		PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired       PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "operator_required"
17		PreClaimIntakeError                  PreClaimIntakeOutcome = "intake_error"
18	)
19	
20	// PreClaimIntakeResult is the typed decision returned by the intake hook.
21	// Detail carries the human-readable rationale or infra failure context.
22	type PreClaimIntakeResult struct {
23		Outcome      PreClaimIntakeOutcome `json:"outcome,omitempty"`
24		Reason       string                `json:"reason,omitempty"`
25		SystemReason string                `json:"system_reason,omitempty"`
26		Detail       string                `json:"detail,omitempty"`
27		Rewrite      PreClaimIntakeRewrite `json:"rewrite,omitempty"`
28		// Decomposition is populated when Outcome == too_large_decomposed and the
29		// hook produced a concrete split with children and AC map.
30		Decomposition *PreClaimDecomposition `json:"decomposition,omitempty"`
31	}
32	
33	// PreClaimDecompositionChild is one proposed child bead in a splitter result.
34	type PreClaimDecompositionChild struct {
35		Title       string   `json:"title"`
36		Description string   `json:"description"`
37		Acceptance  string   `json:"acceptance"`
38		Labels      []string `json:"labels,omitempty"`
39	}
40	
41	// ACMapEntry maps one parent acceptance criterion to child coverage.
42	// Coverage must be non-empty; use "operator_required" or "non_scope" for
43	// ACs that cannot be expressed as a child bead.
44	type ACMapEntry struct {
45		ParentAC string `json:"parent_ac"` // text of the parent AC
46		Coverage string `json:"coverage"`  // child AC text | "operator_required" | "non_scope"
47	}
48	
49	// PreClaimDecomposition is the splitter output when outcome is too_large_decomposed.
50	// Every parent AC must have a corresponding ACMap entry with non-empty Coverage;
51	// a split with missing or empty Coverage entries is lossy and blocks for operator.
52	type PreClaimDecomposition struct {
53		Children  []PreClaimDecompositionChild `json:"children"`
54		ACMap     []ACMapEntry                 `json:"ac_map"`
55		Rationale string                       `json:"rationale"`
56	}
57	
58	// isDecompositionLossy returns true if any ACMapEntry has empty Coverage,
59	// indicating the split does not fully cover all parent acceptance criteria.
60	func isDecompositionLossy(acMap []ACMapEntry) bool {
61		for _, entry := range acMap {
62			if strings.TrimSpace(entry.Coverage) == "" {
63				return true
64			}
65		}
66		return false
67	}
68	
69	// PreClaimIntakeRewrite carries intent-preserving bead edits returned by the
70	// intake hook when a bead is actionable but needs safe refinement before
71	// claim.

73		Description   string   `json:"description,omitempty"`
74		Acceptance    string   `json:"acceptance,omitempty"`
75		ChangedFields []string `json:"changed_fields,omitempty"`
76	}

79	// actionable_atomic as pass-through, skips Claim for non-atomic outcomes, and
80	// fail-opens when the hook returns an infrastructure error.
81	type PreClaimIntakeHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (PreClaimIntakeResult, error)

84		switch r.Outcome {

87		case PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten, PreClaimIntakeTooLargeDecomposed,
88			PreClaimIntakeOperatorRequired, PreClaimIntakeError:
89			return r.Outcome
90		default:
91			return PreClaimIntakeError
92		}

13	// newBeadAcCheckCommand wires `ddx bead ac-check <id>` — mechanical per-AC
14	// verification (test-name match, symbol grep, negative-AC absence, mechanical
15	// file detection). Prose AC items yield needs_judgment, never auto-pass or
16	// auto-fail. The output is consumed by the reviewer at the gate so it adjudicates
17	// structured input instead of grepping a raw diff.
18	func (f *CommandFactory) newBeadAcCheckCommand() *cobra.Command {
19		cmd := &cobra.Command{
20			Use:   "ac-check <bead-id>",
21			Short: "Mechanically verify acceptance criteria against the working tree",
22			Long: `ac-check classifies each AC into a kind (test-name | build-gate |
23	negative | symbol | mechanical | prose) and gathers evidence
24	mechanically. Output is emitted as JSON to stdout (when --json) and to
25	.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when --attempt-id is given.
26	
27	Examples:
28	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123
29	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --rev-range main..HEAD --json
30	  ddx bead ac-check ddx-abc123 --attempt-id 20260510T210000-abcdef00`,
31			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
32			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
33				beadID := args[0]
34				s := f.beadStore()
35				b, err := s.Get(beadID)
36				if err != nil {
37					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
38				}
39				if b == nil {
40					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s not found", beadID)
41				}
42	
43				items := accheck.ParseAcceptance(b.Acceptance)
44				if len(items) == 0 {
45					return fmt.Errorf("bead %s has no acceptance criteria to check", beadID)
46				}
47	
48				revRange, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("rev-range")
49				attemptID, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("attempt-id")
50				asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
51				packages, _ := cmd.Flags().GetStringSlice("package")
52	
53				workspace := f.beadWorkspaceRoot()
54				if workspace == "" {
55					workspace = f.WorkingDir
56				}
57	
58				ctx := accheck.Context{
59					WorkingDir: workspace,
60					RevBase:    revRange,
61					Packages:   packages,
62					RunTest:    accheck.DefaultRunTest(workspace),
63					GitGrep:    accheck.DefaultGitGrep(workspace),
64					DiffHits:   accheck.DefaultDiffHits(workspace, revRange),
65				}
66	
67				entries := accheck.Evaluate(items, ctx)
68				out := accheck.Aggregate(beadID, attemptID, entries)
69	
70				// Persist to .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/ac-check.json when we have one.
71				if attemptID != "" {
72					path := filepath.Join(workspace, ".ddx", "executions", attemptID, "ac-check.json")
73					if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0o755); err != nil {
74						return fmt.Errorf("mkdir %s: %w", filepath.Dir(path), err)
75					}
76					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
77					if mErr != nil {
78						return fmt.Errorf("marshal ac-check.json: %w", mErr)
79					}
80					if err := os.WriteFile(path, blob, 0o644); err != nil {
81						return fmt.Errorf("write %s: %w", path, err)
82					}
83				}
84	
85				if asJSON {
86					blob, mErr := json.MarshalIndent(out, "", "  ")
87					if mErr != nil {
88						return mErr
89					}
90					fmt.Fprintln(cmd.OutOrStdout(), string(blob))
91					return nil
92				}
93				return accheck.WriteHuman(cmd.OutOrStdout(), out)
94			},
95		}
96		cmd.Flags().String("rev-range", "", "git diff range used to scope DiffHits (e.g., main..HEAD)")
97		cmd.Flags().String("attempt-id", "", "if set, persist ac-check.json under .ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/")
98		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "emit JSON to stdout instead of the human-readable table")
99		cmd.Flags().StringSlice("package", nil, "Go packages to scope test runs to (default ./...)")
100		return cmd
101	}

28	// NewTestConfigForLoop returns a *Config that, when Resolve()d with the

package agent

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"testing"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func newACQualityTestStore(t *testing.T) *bead.Store {
	t.Helper()
	store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
	require.NoError(t, store.Init())
	return store
}

func newACQualityBead(t *testing.T, store *bead.Store, acceptance string) *bead.Bead {
	t.Helper()
	b := &bead.Bead{
		Title:      "AC quality test bead",
		Acceptance: acceptance,
	}
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))
	return b
}

func TestPreClaimACQuality_AllMechanicalACs_Passes(t *testing.T) {
	store := newACQualityTestStore(t)
	// TestFoo → test-name, go test → build-gate: all verifiable
	b := newACQualityBead(t, store,
		"1. TestFooCreates passes\n2. TestBarDeletes passes\n3. cd cli && go test ./... green")

	eligible, result, err := RunPreClaimACQualityLint(store, b.ID, DefaultACQualityMinScore)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.True(t, eligible, "all verifiable ACs must be eligible")
	assert.Equal(t, 1.0, result.Score)
	assert.Equal(t, result.Total, result.Verifiable)

	got, err := store.Get(b.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.NotEqual(t, false, got.Extra[bead.ExtraExecutionElig], "must not be marked ineligible")
}

func TestPreClaimACQuality_AllProseACs_BlocksClaim(t *testing.T) {
	store := newACQualityTestStore(t)
	// Pure prose — no mechanical anchor
	b := newACQualityBead(t, store,
		"1. improve code quality\n2. make logs cleaner\n3. enhance readability overall")

	eligible, result, err := RunPreClaimACQualityLint(store, b.ID, DefaultACQualityMinScore)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.False(t, eligible, "all prose ACs must block claim")
	assert.Equal(t, 0.0, result.Score)
	assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Verifiable)

	got, err := store.Get(b.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, false, got.Extra[bead.ExtraExecutionElig], "execution-eligible must be false")
	assert.Contains(t, got.Labels, "ac-quality:needs-refinement")
}

func TestPreClaimACQuality_MixedACs_ScoreAboveThreshold_Passes(t *testing.T) {
	store := newACQualityTestStore(t)
	// 2 verifiable (test-name + build-gate), 1 prose → score ≈ 0.67 > 0.5
	b := newACQualityBead(t, store,
		"1. TestFooWorks passes\n2. cd cli && go test ./internal/... green\n3. improve overall readability")

	eligible, result, err := RunPreClaimACQualityLint(store, b.ID, DefaultACQualityMinScore)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.True(t, eligible, "score above threshold must be eligible")
	assert.Greater(t, result.Score, DefaultACQualityMinScore)

	got, err := store.Get(b.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.NotEqual(t, false, got.Extra[bead.ExtraExecutionElig], "must not be marked ineligible")
}

func TestPreClaimACQuality_MixedACs_ScoreBelowThreshold_Routes(t *testing.T) {
	store := newACQualityTestStore(t)
	// 1 verifiable (test-name), 3 prose → score = 0.25 < 0.5
	b := newACQualityBead(t, store,
		"1. TestFooWorks passes\n2. improve logging\n3. enhance clarity\n4. make it better overall")

	eligible, result, err := RunPreClaimACQualityLint(store, b.ID, DefaultACQualityMinScore)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.False(t, eligible, "score below threshold must route bead to refinement")
	assert.Less(t, result.Score, DefaultACQualityMinScore)

	got, err := store.Get(b.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, false, got.Extra[bead.ExtraExecutionElig], "execution-eligible must be false")
	assert.Contains(t, got.Labels, "ac-quality:needs-refinement")
}

func TestPreClaimACQuality_LowQualityEmitsACQualityEvent(t *testing.T) {
	store := newACQualityTestStore(t)
	b := newACQualityBead(t, store,
		"1. improve readability\n2. make it better")

	_, result, err := RunPreClaimACQualityLint(store, b.ID, DefaultACQualityMinScore)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	events, err := store.Events(b.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	var found *bead.BeadEvent
	for i := range events {
		if events[i].Kind == "ac-quality-low" {
			found = &events[i]
			break
		}
	}
	require.NotNil(t, found, "ac-quality-low event must be emitted")

	var payload ACQualityResult
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(found.Body), &payload),
		"event body must be JSON-decodable ACQualityResult")
	assert.Equal(t, result.Score, payload.Score)
	assert.Equal(t, result.Total, payload.Total)
	assert.Equal(t, result.Verifiable, payload.Verifiable)
	assert.Len(t, payload.Classes, result.Total)
}

package cmd

import (
	"encoding/json"
	"os"
	"path/filepath"
	"strings"
	"testing"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestBeadLint_CLI_PrintsClassification(t *testing.T) {
	workingDir := t.TempDir()
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx"), 0o755))

	factory := newBeadTestRoot(t, workingDir)
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()

	// Initialize bead storage
	_, err := executeCommand(root, "bead", "init")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	// Create a bead with mixed acceptance criteria:
	// 2 verifiable (TestFoo → test-name, go test → build-gate), 1 prose
	acceptance := "1. TestFooCreates passes\n2. cd cli && go test ./... green\n3. improve overall code clarity"
	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(workingDir, ".ddx"))
	b := &bead.Bead{
		Title:      "bead lint test subject",
		Acceptance: acceptance,
	}
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(b))

	// Human-readable output includes the score and per-AC classification
	out, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "lint", b.ID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Contains(t, out, "score:", "output must include score")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "AC #1", "output must list per-AC classification")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "AC #2", "output must list per-AC classification")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "AC #3", "output must list per-AC classification")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "test-name", "test-name AC must be classified correctly")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "build-gate", "build-gate AC must be classified correctly")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "prose", "prose AC must be classified correctly")

	// JSON output includes structured fields
	jsonOut, err := executeCommand(factory.NewRootCommand(), "bead", "lint", b.ID, "--json")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	var result struct {
		BeadID     string `json:"bead_id"`
		Score      float64 `json:"score"`
		Verifiable int     `json:"verifiable"`
		Total      int     `json:"total"`
		MinScore   float64 `json:"min_score"`
		Eligible   bool    `json:"eligible"`
		Classes    []struct {
			AC   int    `json:"ac"`
			Kind string `json:"kind"`
			Text string `json:"text"`
		} `json:"classes"`
	}
	require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(strings.TrimSpace(jsonOut)), &result))
	assert.Equal(t, b.ID, result.BeadID)
	assert.Equal(t, 3, result.Total)
	assert.Equal(t, 2, result.Verifiable)
	assert.InDelta(t, 0.667, result.Score, 0.001)
	assert.Equal(t, 0.5, result.MinScore)
	assert.True(t, result.Eligible, "score 0.67 is above threshold 0.5")
	assert.Len(t, result.Classes, 3)
}

1. **AC #1** — `cli/internal/agent/acqualitylint.go` (`ComputeACQuality` + `RunPreClaimACQualityLint`) computes `verifiable_count / total` score via `accheck.ParseAcceptance`.

3. **AC #3** — When score < threshold: sets `Extra["execution-eligible"] = false`, adds `"ac-quality:needs-refinement"` label, emits `"ac-quality-low"` event with JSON body.
4. **AC #4** — `ddx bead lint <id>` in `cli/cmd/bead_lint.go` prints per-AC classification and score; `--json` outputs structured `lintOutput`.
5. **AC #5** — All 6 named tests pass.
6. **AC #6** — `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/...` green.
